places.name: Intramuros (Walled City)
places.description: The historic walled city of Manila, built by the Spanish in the late 16th century. Its cobblestone streets are lined with well-preserved colonial-era architecture, old churches, and fortifications. Key sights within Intramuros include Fort Santiago, San Agustin Church (a UNESCO World Heritage Site), Manila Cathedral, Casa Manila Museum, and Baluarte de San Diego. Walking through Intramuros feels like stepping back in time, with horse-drawn carriages (kalesa) adding to the old-world ambiance. The district also houses quaint cafes, souvenir shops, and several small museums, making it a must-visit for history buffs and first-time visitors.
places.entrance_fee: Free (general area); individual sites inside charge varying fees (e.g., Fort Santiago PHP 75, Casa Manila PHP 75, San Agustin Museum PHP 200)
places.operating_hours: Open 24 hours (public streets); most museums and attractions open Tue–Sun, 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
places.address: Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Early morning (8:00 AM – 10:00 AM) to avoid heat and crowds; November–February for cooler weather

places.name: Fort Santiago
places.description: A 16th-century citadel located at the mouth of the Pasig River within Intramuros. It served as a defense fortress during the Spanish colonial period and later as a prison, most famously holding national hero José Rizal before his execution in 1896. Today, visitors can explore the well-preserved gates, ramparts, dungeons, and a small museum dedicated to Rizal, which houses memorabilia including his personal belongings and writings. The site offers scenic views of the Pasig River and the Manila skyline, as well as lush gardens and open courtyards perfect for leisurely strolls.
places.operating_hours: Mon–Fri: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM (last entry 8:00 PM); Sat–Sun: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM (last entry 8:30 PM)
places.address: Sta. Clara St., Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for fewer crowds; late afternoon for golden-hour photos

places.name: San Agustin Church and Museum
places.description: The oldest stone church in the Philippines, completed in 1607 and recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Its Baroque architecture has withstood earthquakes, typhoons, and wars for over four centuries. The interior features a stunning trompe-l'œil ceiling, ornate altars, and a 17th-century pipe organ. Adjacent to the church, the San Agustin Museum occupies the former monastery and displays religious art, antique vestments, colonial-era furniture, and a collection of 16th-century Chinese porcelain. The choir loft on the second floor offers a breathtaking panoramic view of the church interior.
places.operating_hours: Church: Daily, 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Museum: Daily, 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM
places.address: Gen. Luna St. cor. Real St., Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for quiet contemplation; attend a mass for a more immersive experience

places.name: Manila Cathedral (Minor Basilica and Metropolitan Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception)
places.description: The seat of the Archdiocese of Manila, this Romanesque Revival cathedral has been rebuilt eight times since its original construction in 1581 due to destruction from earthquakes, typhoons, and wars. The current structure features a striking dome, rose windows, intricate stained glass, a gilded altar, and a 4,500-pipe organ. Located in the heart of Intramuros facing Plaza de Roma, the cathedral is a popular venue for weddings and religious events. Visitors can admire the bronze doors with Latin inscriptions, the serene interior, and the beautifully mosaicked side chapels.
places.operating_hours: Tue–Sat: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Sun: 8:00 AM – 11:30 AM; Closed Mon (Mass schedules vary)
places.address: Cabildo, 132 Beaterio St., Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for a quieter visit; Sunday mornings to witness a full mass service

places.name: Rizal Park (Luneta Park)
places.description: A 58-hectare urban park in the heart of Manila, dedicated to national hero Dr. José Rizal. The park features manicured lawns, ornamental gardens, fountains, and the iconic Rizal Monument, where a changing-of-the-guard ceremony takes place daily. Within the park are the Chinese Garden, Japanese Garden, an orchidarium, a planetarium, and several open-air event spaces. It is a popular spot for picnics, kite flying, jogging, and evening strolls. The park also hosts concerts, cultural performances, and national celebrations throughout the year.
places.entrance_fee: Free (general park); some attractions inside charge minimal fees
places.operating_hours: Daily, 5:00 AM – 10:00 PM (some sections open 24 hours)
places.address: Roxas Blvd., Ermita, Manila, 1000 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Late afternoon to early evening for cooler temperatures and sunset views; early morning for jogging

places.name: National Museum of Fine Arts
places.description: Housed in a grand neoclassical building along Padre Burgos Avenue, this museum serves as the country's premier repository of Filipino visual arts. Its centerpiece is Juan Luna's monumental painting 'Spoliarium,' which won a gold medal at the 1884 Madrid Exposition. The collection spans from classical to contemporary works, including masterpieces by Felix Resurrección Hidalgo, Fernando Amorsolo, and Guillermo Tolentino. The museum also features rotating exhibitions, sculptures, and installations that tell the story of Philippine history through art.
places.address: Padre Burgos Ave., Ermita, Manila, 1000 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for a quieter, more contemplative experience; allow 2–3 hours to explore

places.name: National Museum of Anthropology
places.description: Located across from the National Museum of Fine Arts, this museum focuses on the cultural and ethnic diversity of the Philippines. Its exhibits include artifacts from indigenous groups, ancient weaponry, traditional textiles, Baybayin (ancient script) artifacts, and maritime trade relics. Highlights include the 'San Diego: 500 Years of Maritime Trade' exhibit, which showcases treasures recovered from a 17th-century Spanish galleon, and a gallery dedicated to the country's ethnography, featuring portraits and cultural items of indigenous women and communities.
places.address: Padre Burgos Ave., Ermita, Manila, 1000 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday afternoons; combine with a visit to the National Museum of Fine Arts for a full cultural immersion

places.name: National Museum of Natural History
places.description: The newest addition to the National Museum complex, this museum showcases the Philippines' rich biodiversity. The striking building features a 'Tree of Life' elevator structure at its center and houses over 10 galleries filled with specimens of flora, fauna, and geological wonders. Visitors can see a replica of Lolong, the largest crocodile ever held in captivity, as well as exhibits on the Philippine Eagle, the Tamaraw, marine ecosystems, and rainforest habitats. The museum is both educational and visually captivating, making it a favorite among families and nature enthusiasts.
places.address: Teodoro F. Valencia Circle, Ermita, Manila, 1000 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings; allow 2–3 hours to fully explore the multiple floors

places.name: Ayala Museum
places.description: A modern, privately-run museum located in the Ayala Center in Makati, just outside Manila proper. It features six floors of permanent and temporary exhibitions, including a renowned collection of 60 handcrafted dioramas depicting key moments in Philippine history, pre-colonial gold artifacts, Chinese porcelain, and works by Filipino masters such as Fernando Zóbel and Juan Luna. The museum also hosts contemporary art exhibitions, cultural events, and educational workshops. Its sleek design and interactive displays make it a refreshing contrast to the older national museums.
places.entrance_fee: PHP 425 (adults); discounted rates for students and seniors
places.operating_hours: Tue–Fri: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Sat–Sun: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM; Closed Mon
places.address: Makati Avenue corner De La Rosa Street, Greenbelt Park, Ayala Center, Makati, 1224 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday afternoons; combine with shopping at nearby Greenbelt Mall

places.name: Casa Manila
places.description: A faithful replica of a 19th-century Spanish colonial mansion located within Intramuros. The two-story house showcases the opulent lifestyle of the Filipino elite during the Spanish period, with period furniture, a carved four-poster bed, a long dining table, ceramic jars in the kitchen, and other authentic decor. The courtyard features a beautiful fountain and tropical plants, creating a serene escape. Guided tours are available, providing insights into colonial domestic life, architecture, and social customs.
places.operating_hours: Tue–Sun: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM (last entry 5:00 PM); Closed Mon
places.address: Real St. cor. Gen. Luna St., Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for a quieter tour; combine with Fort Santiago and San Agustin

places.name: Manila Ocean Park
places.description: An oceanarium and marine-themed park located behind the Quirino Grandstand at Rizal Park. Spanning 8,000 square meters, it houses thousands of marine animals from the Philippines and Southeast Asia. Main attractions include the Oceanarium with a 25-meter acrylic tunnel walkway, the Jellies Exhibit, the Trails to Antarctica featuring penguins, a Sea Lion Show, and the Birds of Prey Kingdom. The park also offers interactive experiences such as mermaid swimming shows, a fish spa, and a hotel with underwater-view rooms. It's a family-friendly destination combining entertainment with marine conservation education.
places.entrance_fee: PHP 3,300 (full access pass); various package options available
places.operating_hours: Daily: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM (hours may vary seasonally)
places.address: Behind Quirino Grandstand, Ermita, Manila, 1000 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekdays to avoid crowds; arrive at opening time to maximize your visit

places.name: Binondo (Chinatown)
places.description: The oldest Chinatown in the world, established in 1594. Binondo is a bustling district known for its rich history, vibrant streets, and, most importantly, its incredible food scene. Ongpin Street is the main artery, lined with Chinese restaurants, bakeries, herbal shops, and gold and jewelry stores. Foodies flock here for fried siopao, hand-pulled noodles, dim sum, hopia (bean-filled pastries), and other Chinese-Filipino delicacies. Key landmarks include the Binondo Church (Minor Basilica of St. Lorenzo Ruiz) and the Chinese-Philippine Friendship Gate. The area is best explored on foot with an empty stomach and a sense of culinary adventure.
places.operating_hours: Most restaurants and shops open daily from 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM (varies per establishment)
places.address: Binondo, Manila, 1006 Metro Manila, Philippines (main hub along Ongpin St.)
places.best_time_visit: Late morning to early afternoon for a food crawl; avoid Sunday evenings when some shops close early

places.name: Arroceros Forest Park
places.description: A 2.7-hectare urban oasis along the Pasig River, often called the 'Last Lung of Manila.' The park is home to over 60 tree species, some centuries old, and serves as a habitat for migratory birds and various wildlife. Well-maintained pathways wind through lush greenery, leading to a riverside promenade with benches and viewing decks. Despite its small size, the park provides a tranquil escape from the city's noise and pollution. It's ideal for nature walks, birdwatching, reading, or simply enjoying fresh air in the middle of the metropolis.
places.address: 659 A. Antonio Villegas St., Ermita, Manila, 1000 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Early morning for birdwatching; weekdays for solitude

places.name: Manila Baywalk
places.description: A scenic promenade along Roxas Boulevard facing Manila Bay, famous for its breathtaking sunsets. The Baywalk stretches for several kilometers and is lined with benches, palm trees, food stalls, and occasional street performers. Locals and tourists gather here in the late afternoon to watch the sky turn brilliant shades of orange, pink, and purple as the sun dips below the horizon. Vendors sell local snacks like taho (sweet tofu), fish balls, and buko (coconut) juice. Nearby are the Cultural Center of the Philippines, the Philippine International Convention Center, and several hotels and restaurants with bay views.
places.operating_hours: Open 24 hours (best visited from 4:00 PM – 7:00 PM)
places.address: Roxas Blvd., Malate, Manila, 1004 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Late afternoon to sunset (5:00 PM – 6:30 PM) for the best views and cooler weather

places.name: Quiapo Church (Minor Basilica of the Black Nazarene)
places.description: A Roman Catholic basilica in the bustling district of Quiapo, home to the revered life-sized statue of the Black Nazarene, believed by devotees to be miraculous. Every Friday, thousands of worshippers flock to the church for novena masses, and the annual Feast of the Black Nazarene on January 9 draws millions in one of the world's largest religious processions. The church's architecture blends Baroque and modern elements, and its location in the heart of Quiapo makes it a vibrant center of faith and commerce. Nearby, Plaza Miranda and the busy Quiapo Market add to the area's lively atmosphere.
places.address: Plaza Miranda, Quezon Blvd., Quiapo, Manila, 1001 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekdays for a quieter visit; Friday for a more intense devotional experience

places.name: San Sebastian Church (Minor Basilica of San Sebastian)
places.description: The only all-steel church in Asia, this Gothic Revival basilica was completed in 1891 and is recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Prefabricated in Belgium and assembled in Manila, the church features intricate steel vaulting, stained glass windows, and twin spires. Its interior is richly painted to resemble marble and stone, creating a breathtaking visual effect. The church is dedicated to Our Lady of Mount Carmel and houses a revered image of the Virgin. Ongoing restoration efforts aim to preserve this unique architectural and historical treasure.
places.address: Plaza del Carmen, Recto Ave., Quiapo, Manila, 1001 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for photography and quiet reflection; combine with a visit to Quiapo Church

places.name: SM Mall of Asia
places.description: One of the largest shopping malls in the Philippines and the world, located on a reclaimed area along Manila Bay in Pasay City. The complex includes hundreds of retail shops, a wide range of restaurants and cafes, an IMAX theater, a concert arena, a convention center, and an amusement park featuring a giant Ferris wheel. The bay-side promenade offers stunning sunset views and is lined with bars and seafood restaurants. It's a one-stop destination for shopping, dining, entertainment, and leisure.
places.address: Seaside Blvd., Mall of Asia Complex, Pasay, 1300 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday afternoons to avoid heavy weekend crowds; evenings for sunset views and cooler temperatures

places.name: Greenbelt Mall
places.description: An upscale shopping and lifestyle complex in the heart of Makati's business district. Greenbelt consists of five interconnected buildings set around a lush central park with ponds, a chapel, and landscaped gardens. The mall houses luxury boutiques, international brands, fine dining restaurants, cafes, and art galleries. It's a popular destination for both shopping and dining, offering a more relaxed, open-air atmosphere compared to other malls in the metro. The surrounding Greenbelt area is also known for its vibrant nightlife and cultural venues.
places.operating_hours: Sun–Thu: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Fri–Sat: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
places.address: Legazpi Street, Legazpi Village, Makati, 1229 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday afternoons for quieter shopping; evenings for al fresco dining and nightlife

places.name: Divisoria Market
places.description: The ultimate bargain shopping destination in Manila, Divisoria is a sprawling district filled with malls, street stalls, and warehouses selling almost everything imaginable—clothing, accessories, home goods, electronics, toys, and school supplies—at wholesale and retail prices. The area is famously chaotic, with narrow streets crowded by shoppers, vendors, and tricycles. It's a paradise for frugal shoppers and those looking to experience authentic local commerce. Popular spots include 168 Mall, 999 Mall, and Tutuban Center. Bargaining is expected, and patience is rewarded with incredible deals.
places.operating_hours: Most stalls and malls open daily: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM (best to visit mornings)
places.address: Divisoria, Tondo, Manila, 1012 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for the best selection and fewer crowds; avoid the holiday season rush

places.name: Quinta Market (Quiapo Market)
places.description: Also known as Quiapo Market, this public market is located along the Pasig River in the Quiapo district. Renovated and modernized in 2017, Quinta Market is renowned for its fresh seafood, exotic ingredients, and local Filipino produce. It's a paradise for food enthusiasts looking to explore authentic Filipino cuisine, offering everything from fresh fish and meats to tropical fruits, spices, and cooked street food. The market also has a section for dry goods and household items. Adjacent to the market is the Quiapo Church, making it a convenient stop for visitors exploring the area.
places.address: Carlos Palanca St., Quiapo, Manila, 1001 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Early morning for the freshest produce and seafood; weekdays for a less crowded experience

places.name: Ilustrado Restaurant
places.description: A heritage restaurant located within Intramuros, Ilustrado offers a romantic dining experience in a beautifully restored colonial building with antique decor, ambient lighting, and a lush courtyard. The menu features a fusion of Filipino and Spanish cuisine, with signature dishes such as paella, callos (stewed ox tripe), lengua estofada (braised ox tongue), and Caesar salad. It's a popular venue for weddings, special occasions, and romantic dinners, providing old-world charm and excellent service in the heart of historic Manila.
places.operating_hours: Mon–Sat: 8:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Sun: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
places.address: 744 General Luna St., Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday lunch for a quieter meal; evening for a romantic candlelit dinner

places.name: The Aristocrat Restaurant
places.description: A beloved Filipino institution since the 1930s, The Aristocrat is famous for its Filipino-style barbecue chicken, which has become an iconic comfort food in Manila. The restaurant offers a wide menu of Filipino classics, including crispy pata, kare-kare (oxtail stew in peanut sauce), lumpia (spring rolls), and halo-halo (shaved ice dessert). With its casual, family-friendly atmosphere and rich history, The Aristocrat has served generations of Manileños and remains a top choice for authentic local flavors.
places.address: 432 San Andres St., Malate, Manila, 1004 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Late lunch or early dinner; weekends for a lively, bustling atmosphere

places.name: Dong Bei Dumplings (Binondo)
places.description: A popular hole-in-the-wall eatery in Binondo specializing in freshly made Chinese dumplings. The unassuming restaurant serves plump, juicy pork-and-chive dumplings boiled to perfection and served with black vinegar and soy dipping sauce. Other menu highlights include hand-pulled noodles and scallion pancakes. The no-frills setting and affordable prices make it a favorite among locals and tourists on a Binondo food crawl.
places.address: 642 Yuchengco St., Binondo, Manila, 1006 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Off-peak hours (around 11:00 AM or 3:00 PM) to avoid long queues

places.name: Lan Zhou La Mien (Binondo)
places.description: A renowned noodle house in Binondo famous for its hand-pulled lamian noodles, made fresh to order by skilled chefs who stretch and pull the dough in full view of diners. The beef noodle soup is the star, featuring springy noodles in a rich, flavorful broth topped with tender beef slices and vegetables. The menu also includes fried rice, dumplings, and various Chinese side dishes. Its authentic flavors and affordable prices make it a must-visit on any Chinatown food tour.
places.address: 818 Benavidez St., Binondo, Manila, 1006 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Late morning or mid-afternoon to avoid peak lunch and dinner crowds

places.name: El Poco Cantina (Malate)
places.description: A small, casual Mexican restaurant in Malate that has earned a loyal following and Michelin recognition for its outstanding birria tacos—crispy, savory, and packed with flavor. The menu features affordable yet high-quality Mexican street food, including quesabirria, elotes, and refreshing margaritas. The vibrant, no-frills atmosphere and friendly service make it a go-to spot for a satisfying, budget-friendly meal in the heart of Manila.
places.address: Malate, Manila, 1004 Metro Manila, Philippines (near Adriatico St.)
places.best_time_visit: Weekday evenings for a relaxed dinner; weekends for a livelier vibe

places.name: Manam Comfort Filipino
places.description: A trendy Filipino restaurant chain that puts a modern twist on classic comfort food. Manam offers two sizes for most dishes—'small' (perfect for sampling) and 'large' (for sharing)—making it ideal for groups wanting to try a variety of flavors. Signature dishes include house crispy sisig, sinigang na beef short rib with watermelon, and ube + sago (purple yam pudding). The stylish yet cozy interiors and reasonable prices make it a favorite among locals, families, and foodies.
places.address: Multiple branches in Manila, including Robinsons Place Manila, Ermita
places.best_time_visit: Weekday lunch for shorter wait times; dinner for a livelier atmosphere

places.name: City of Dreams Manila
places.description: A premier integrated resort and casino complex in Entertainment City, Parañaque, just outside Manila proper. City of Dreams features three luxury hotels (Nobu, Hyatt Regency, and Nüwa), a casino, high-end retail shops, and a wide array of dining options ranging from celebrity-chef restaurants to casual cafes. The highlight for families is DreamPlay, an interactive theme park by DreamWorks with attractions based on popular animated movies. The complex also hosts live entertainment shows, nightclubs, and bars, making it a one-stop destination for luxury, fun, and entertainment.
places.entrance_fee: Free entry (DreamPlay and some attractions require separate tickets)
places.address: Asean Avenue corner Roxas Boulevard, Entertainment City, Parañaque, 1701 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Evenings for nightlife and dining; weekdays for fewer crowds

places.name: Okada Manila
places.description: An opulent integrated resort and casino in Entertainment City, known for its grand architecture, lavish interiors, and world-class entertainment. The highlight is 'The Fountain'—a spectacular outdoor dancing water show set to lights and music, among the largest in the world. The resort also features a luxury hotel, multiple pools, Cove Manila (a beach club with a retractable roof), a spa, fine dining restaurants, and a sprawling casino. Okada's PLAY children's center offers supervised indoor activities, making the resort suitable for family visits as well.
places.entrance_fee: Free entry (fountain show is free; casino, dining, and PLAY have separate charges)
places.operating_hours: Open 24 hours (individual venue hours vary; fountain show typically runs every 30 minutes in the evening)
places.address: New Seaside Drive, Entertainment City, Parañaque, 1701 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Early evening to catch the fountain show and enjoy dinner; weekdays for a more relaxed experience

places.name: Poblacion Nightlife District (Makati)
places.description: The hippest nightlife hub in Metro Manila, Poblacion is a maze of narrow streets packed with bars, speakeasies, live music venues, hostels, and international restaurants. The area has a gritty, bohemian charm, attracting a mix of locals, expats, and tourists. Popular spots include The Spirits Library (a hidden bar with craft cocktails and rare spirits), The Bolthole (a trendy nightclub), and El Chupacabra (a dive bar serving cheap Mexican food and drinks). The area is also known for its live indie music scene and pop-up events, making it the go-to destination for a memorable night out.
places.entrance_fee: Varies per establishment (most have no cover charge)
places.operating_hours: Most bars and clubs open from 5:00 PM – 2:00 AM (some until 5:00 AM)
places.address: Poblacion, Makati, 1210 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Friday and Saturday nights for the liveliest scene; arrive early to find parking and secure a table

places.name: Baluarte de San Diego
places.description: A well-preserved 16th-century bastion and circular fortress within Intramuros, built as part of the city's defensive walls. The site features a sunken garden surrounded by stone ruins, arches, and walkways, with vibrant bougainvillea adding color to the old stonework. The gardens are a peaceful retreat within the walled city, perfect for photography, quiet reflection, or a romantic stroll. The bastion offers a glimpse into Manila's colonial military history and is less crowded than Fort Santiago.
places.address: Sta. Lucia St. cor. Muralla St., Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Late afternoon for soft lighting and fewer visitors; weekdays for solitude

places.name: Museo Pambata (Children's Museum)
places.description: An interactive children's museum housed in a former civic building along Roxas Boulevard. Museo Pambata (meaning 'Museum for Children') features hands-on exhibits that teach kids about Filipino culture, science, history, and the environment through play. Sections include a simulated marketplace, a traditional Filipino home, a science discovery room, and a theater. The museum is designed to be engaging for both children and adults, making it a perfect family outing destination.
places.entrance_fee: PHP 250 (adults and children); discounts for groups
places.operating_hours: Tue–Sat: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sun: 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Closed Mon
places.address: Roxas Blvd. cor. South Drive, Ermita, Manila, 1000 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings for a less crowded experience; weekends for special programs and activities

places.name: Manila Zoo
places.description: Recently renovated and reopened, Manila Zoo is one of the oldest zoos in Asia, located in the Malate district. The zoo houses a variety of animals, including Mali, the lone Asian elephant and one of its most famous residents, as well as lions, tigers, monkeys, birds, and reptiles. The renovated facilities include improved enclosures, a botanical garden, a playground, and picnic areas. It's a popular destination for families and school field trips, offering a fun and educational experience for children.
places.entrance_fee: PHP 300 (adults); PHP 200 (children); discounts for Manila residents
places.address: Adriatico St., Malate, Manila, 1004 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday mornings when animals are most active and crowds are smaller

places.name: Paco Park
places.description: A historic circular park and former municipal cemetery built in 1822, where national hero Dr. José Rizal was originally buried before his remains were transferred to Rizal Park. The park features a beautiful domed chapel (St. Pancratius Chapel) at its center, surrounded by thick stone walls, arched walkways, and well-tended gardens. It is a popular venue for weddings, concerts, and quiet afternoon strolls. The serene atmosphere and historical significance make it a hidden gem in the city.
places.address: General Luna St., Paco, Manila, 1007 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Late afternoon for cooler weather; Friday evenings for 'Paco Park Presents' live classical music

places.name: Bahay Tsinoy (Museum of Chinese in Philippine Life)
places.description: A museum located in Intramuros that documents the history, culture, and contributions of the Chinese-Filipino community to Philippine society. Housed in a heritage building, Bahay Tsinoy features life-sized dioramas, artifacts, photographs, and documents tracing the journey of Chinese immigrants from pre-colonial trade to modern-day integration. Exhibits cover topics such as the Chinese role in the Philippine Revolution, prominent Chinese-Filipino figures, and the unique blend of Chinese and Filipino traditions. It's an insightful and often overlooked museum that adds depth to any Intramuros tour.
places.address: 32 Anda St., Intramuros, Manila, 1002 Metro Manila, Philippines
places.best_time_visit: Weekday afternoons; combine with Casa Manila and other Intramuros museums
